stem: A retail study measures customer satisfaction score across 3 chains, each containing multiple stores. chain A has 5 observations with mean 84.0 /10 (within-chain SD = 4.7). chain B has 20 observations with mean 75.6 /10 (within-chain SD = 6.5). chain C has 15 observations with mean 88.3 /10 (within-chain SD = 5.4). The between-chain standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 4.1 and 10.4 /10. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true customer satisfaction score for chain A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-chain variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for chain A: 83.8 /10 (shrunk from raw 84.0). Range [83.5, 83.9] as between-chain SD varies from 4.1 to 10.4. Pooled mean: 81.4.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 81.4 /10 (weighted average of 3 chain means).
Shrinkage factor for chain A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=6.9: B = 0.915.
Interval [83.5, 83.9] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (84.0) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (81.4) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
